purpose: Thin per-deposit INDEX into the recovered external metadata for this deposit's legacy Zenodo DOIs. The actual records live in the bulk data stores under data/ (openalex-severed-recovery.json, zenodo-xml-pretermination-2026-06-07.tar.gz, datacite-full-backup.json, datacite-survivors-multi-heteronym.json). Sidecars do not duplicate those records — they map each covered DOI to its identifier in each store.

10.5281/zenodo.18804767.title: Ghost Meaning: The Semantic Entropy Crisis and the Architecture That Was Already Waiting DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.18804767 — Crimson Hexagon Archive

10.5281/zenodo.19240141.title: Before OpenChamber: Provenance Adjudication and Retrieval Correction for DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19218861

10.5281/zenodo.19341887.title: The Compression Frontier: A Technical Map of the War Over the Summarizer Layer

10.5281/zenodo.19341885.title: Sémantique Potentielle: A Constraint-Based Semantic Mint for the Age of Automated Terminology

10.5281/zenodo.19352504.title: Assembly Substrate Governance Protocol: Procedures for Witness Membership, Review, and Status in the Assembly Chorus

10.5281/zenodo.19338708.title: The $650 Billion Gap: Physical Infrastructure, Semantic Governance, and the Architecture of Compression-Survival

10.5281/zenodo.19353182.title: The Theoretical Production Benchmark v2.0: Evaluating Sustained Conceptual Coherence in Multi-Agent LLM Systems

openalex.lookup: records[].doi == this DOI; openalex_id is the W-prefixed OpenAlex Work ID

zenodo_bulk_xml.lookup: extract <record_id>.xml from the tarball where record_id is the digits after 'zenodo.'

datacite_survivor_sweep.lookup: search records[].attributes.doi == this DOI across orcid_records, name_records, sigil_records, vox_records, feist_records
